content.problemStatement:
Suppose you have a **binary** array BBB of length NNN.

A sequence x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xkx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_kx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xk‚Äč is called good with respect to BBB if it satisfies the following conditions:


1‚Č§x1<x2<‚Ä¶<xk‚Č§N+11 \leq x_1 \lt x_2 \lt \ldots \lt x_k \leq N+11‚Č§x1‚Äč<x2‚Äč<‚Ä¶<xk‚Äč‚Č§N+1
For every pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§k1 \leq i \lt j \leq k1‚Č§i<j‚Č§k, the subarray B[xi:xj‚ąí1]B[x_i: x_j-1]B[xi‚Äč:xj‚Äč‚ąí1] contains (j‚ąíi)(j-i)(j‚ąíi) more ones than zeros.

That is, if B[xi:xj‚ąí1]B[x_i : x_j-1]B[xi‚Äč:xj‚Äč‚ąí1] contains c1c_1c1‚Äč ones and c0c_0c0‚Äč zeros, then c1‚ąíc0=j‚ąíic_1 - c_0 = j-ic1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=j‚ąíi must hold.


Here, B[L:R]B[L: R]B[L:R] denotes the subarray consisting of elements [BL,BL+1,BL+2,‚Ä¶,BR][B_L, B_{L+1}, B_{L+2}, \ldots, B_R][BL‚Äč,BL+1‚Äč,BL+2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BR‚Äč].

Note that in particular, a sequence of size 111 is always good.

For example, suppose B=[0,1,1,0,1,1]B = [0,1,1,0,1,1]B=[0,1,1,0,1,1]. Then,


The sequence [1,4,7][1,4,7][1,4,7] is a good sequence. The subarrays that need to be checked are B[1:3],B[1:6]B[1:3], B[1:6]B[1:3],B[1:6] and B[4:6]B[4:6]B[4:6], which all satisfy the condition.
The sequence [1,5][1, 5][1,5] is not good, because B[1:4]=[0,1,1,0]B[1:4] = [0, 1, 1, 0]B[1:4]=[0,1,1,0] contains an equal number of zeros and ones (when it should contain one extra 111).
Alice gave Bob a binary array AAA of size NNN and asked him to find the **longest** sequence that is good with respect to AAA. Help Bob find one such sequence.

If multiple possible longest sequences exist, you may print **any** of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the size of the binary array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated numbers ‚ÄĒ A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1 , A_2 , \ldots , A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
Each test case requires two lines of output:



First, print on a new line a single integer KKK ‚ÄĒ the maximum length of a sequence that is good with respect to AAA
On the next line, print KKK space-separated integers in **increasing order**, denoting the indices of one such sequence.

If there are multiple possible good sequences with maximum size, output any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have A=[0,1,1,0,1,1]A = [0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1]A=[0,1,1,0,1,1]. The sequence [2,5,6,7][2, 5, 6, 7][2,5,6,7] requires us to check 666 subarrays:

A[2:4]=[1,1,0]A[2:4] = [1, 1, 0]A[2:4]=[1,1,0] which has c1‚ąíc0=1c_1 - c_0 = 1c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=1 and corresponds to i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2
A[2:5]=[1,1,0,1]A[2:5] = [1, 1, 0, 1]A[2:5]=[1,1,0,1] which has c1‚ąíc0=2c_1 - c_0 = 2c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=2 and corresponds to i=1,j=3i = 1, j = 3i=1,j=3
A[2:6]=[1,1,0,1,1]A[2:6] = [1, 1, 0, 1, 1]A[2:6]=[1,1,0,1,1] which has c1‚ąíc0=3c_1 - c_0 = 3c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=3 and corresponds to i=1,j=4i = 1, j = 4i=1,j=4
A[5:5]=[1]A[5:5] = [1]A[5:5]=[1], which has c1‚ąíc0=1c_1 - c_0 = 1c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=1 and corresponds to i=2,j=3i = 2, j = 3i=2,j=3
A[5:6]=[1,1]A[5:6] = [1, 1]A[5:6]=[1,1], which has c1‚ąíc0=2c_1 - c_0 = 2c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=2 and corresponds to i=2,j=4i = 2, j = 4i=2,j=4
A[6:6]=[1]A[6:6] = [1]A[6:6]=[1], which has c1‚ąíc0=1c_1 - c_0 = 1c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=1 and corresponds to i=3,j=4i = 3, j = 4i=3,j=4

As you can see, all 666 pairs satisfy the condition.
Test case 222: The only subarray that needs to be checked is A[4:4]=[1]A[4:4] = [1]A[4:4]=[1], which has c1‚ąíc0=1c_1 - c_0 = 1c1‚Äč‚ąíc0‚Äč=1 as needed. It can be verified that no good sequence of length greater than 222 exists.
